fields.name: name
fields.label: Name
fields.helperText: An unique name for the Identity Verification Provider.

fields.name: configProperties.client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.helperText: The OIDC Client ID registered in the Daon TrustX tenant.

fields.name: configProperties.client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.helperText: The OIDC Client Secret registered in the Daon TrustX tenant.

fields.name: configProperties.base_url
fields.label: Base URL
fields.helperText: The Daon TrustX base URL in the format https://{tenant}.oak.trustx.com/auth/realms/{tenant}.

fields.name: configProperties.redirect_uri
fields.label: Redirect URI
fields.helperText: The callback URL registered in Daon TrustX to receive the authorization code.

tabs.guide:
## Daon TrustX Connector Configuration Instructions
***
***
***
This guide outlines the steps to configure the Daon TrustX Connector with ${productName}.
***
***
***
***
### Register an OIDC Client in Daon TrustX
<!--rehype:numbered=true&content=1-->
***
***
Log in to your Daon TrustX administration console and register a new OIDC client. Configure the following URL as the allowed redirect URI for the client.
***
```bash
${redirectUri}
```
***
***
Note down the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** generated for the registered client.
***
***
***
***
### Configure the Daon TrustX connection
<!--rehype:numbered=true&content=2-->
***
***
Navigate to the [Settings tab](#tab=settings)<!--rehype:external=false--> and provide the Client ID, Client Secret, Base URL, Redirect URI, Scope, and Callback URL. Then click on Update to finish the setup.
***
***
***
***
### Configure attribute mappings
<!--rehype:numbered=true&content=3-->
***
***
If you need to provide additional attribute mappings, navigate to the [Attributes tab](#tab=attributes)<!--rehype:external=false--> and provide the attribute mappings. Daon returns verified claims in the OIDC userinfo response using standard OIDC claim names (e.g. `given_name`, `family_name`, `birthdate`).
***
***
***
***
### Integrate Daon TrustX Identity Verification into Your Application
<!--rehype:numbered=true&content=4-->
***
***
To initiate the verification flow, call the WSO2 Identity Server Identity Verification API with the following Identity Verification Provider ID. The API will return an `authorization_url`. Redirect the user's browser to this URL to begin the Daon TrustX identity verification journey.
***
```bash
${identityVerificationProviderId}
```

fields.name: name
fields.label: Name
fields.helperText: An unique name for the Identity Verification Provider.

fields.name: description
fields.label: Description
fields.helperText: A description that will help to identify the Identity Verification Provider.

fields.name: configProperties.client_id
fields.label: Client ID
fields.helperText: The OIDC Client ID registered in the Daon TrustX tenant.

fields.name: configProperties.client_secret
fields.label: Client Secret
fields.helperText: The OIDC Client Secret registered in the Daon TrustX tenant.

fields.name: configProperties.base_url
fields.label: Base URL
fields.helperText: The Daon TrustX base URL in the format https://{tenant}.oak.trustx.com/auth/realms/{tenant}.

fields.name: configProperties.redirect_uri
fields.label: Redirect URI
fields.helperText: The callback URL registered in Daon TrustX to receive the authorization code.

fields.name: configProperties.scope
fields.label: Scope
fields.helperText: Space-separated OIDC scopes to request from Daon TrustX (e.g. openid profile document).

fields.name: configProperties.callback_url
fields.label: Callback URL
fields.helperText: The URL to redirect the user to after the identity verification flow is completed.
